summary: Structural addition (not a weekly diff): created breakthrough #16 'AI dev ecosystem & agent primitives' to track the recurring wave of new developer primitives in the agent stack — MCP -> Agent Skills -> subagents -> agent-orchestration apps -> agent SDKs/evals. This category captures successful/trending tooling (e.g. Conductor) that the paper/product-focused pipeline was missing.

highlights.title: AI dev ecosystem & agent primitives
highlights.detail: New tracked category for the fast-moving layer of agent developer primitives: MCP, Agent Skills, subagents, agent-orchestration apps (Conductor, Intent), agent SDKs, and context-engineering/evals.

payload.title: AI dev ecosystem & agent primitives (MCP, skills, agents, orchestration)

people.name: David Soria Parra
people.role: Member of Technical Staff; co-creator of the Model Context Protocol (MCP)

people.name: Justin Spahr-Summers
people.role: Engineer; co-creator of the Model Context Protocol (MCP)

people.name: Charlie Holtz
people.role: Co-founder & CEO of Conductor (parallel coding-agent orchestration app); previously growth at Replicate

people.name: Jackson de Campos
people.role: Co-founder of Conductor; 'the human interface to AI organizations'

people.name: Harrison Chase
people.role: Founder & CEO of LangChain; creator of LangChain framework and the LangGraph agent-orchestration runtime

people.name: Amelia Wattenberger
people.role: Product Lead for Intent (Augment Code agent-orchestration workspace); Partner, Sutter Hill Ventures; ex-GitHub Next

sub_ideas.name: Model Context Protocol (MCP)
sub_ideas.description: An open standard giving AI assistants a universal two-way way to connect to external data and tools, replacing per-integration connectors with one protocol.
examples: Pre-built MCP servers (Drive, Slack, GitHub, Postgres)
Donated to the Linux Foundation's Agentic AI Foundation (Dec 2025)

sub_ideas.name: Agent Skills (Claude Skills / portable skills)
sub_ideas.description: Modular folders (a SKILL.md plus scripts and resources) that agents discover and load on demand via progressive disclosure to specialize at tasks.

sub_ideas.name: Subagents / multi-agent orchestration in coding agents
sub_ideas.description: Specialized sub-agents that each run in their own context window with custom prompts, tools, and permissions, letting a primary agent delegate and preserve its main context.
examples: Subagents in the Claude Agent SDK

sub_ideas.name: Agent-orchestration desktop/cloud apps
sub_ideas.description: Standalone workspaces to run and coordinate multiple coding agents in parallel across isolated git worktrees, then review and merge their work from one interface.
examples: Conductor (run a team of coding agents on your Mac)

sub_ideas.name: Agent SDKs / frameworks
sub_ideas.description: Libraries and runtimes for building, orchestrating, and deploying multi-agent workflows programmatically.

sub_ideas.name: Context engineering & evals / agent governance
sub_ideas.description: An emerging discipline focused on curating what enters an agent's context window and on systematically evaluating, grading, and governing agent behavior for production reliability.
examples: OpenAI Evals for Agents (trace grading, prompt optimization)

current_frontier.claim: MCP went from an Anthropic experiment (announced Nov 25, 2024) to a cross-industry standard; in Dec 2025 Anthropic donated MCP to the Agentic AI Foundation, a Linux Foundation directed fund co-founded with Block and OpenAI.
Anthropic launched Agent Skills on Oct 16, 2025 across Claude apps, Claude Code, the Agent SDK and the API, then on Dec 18, 2025 published Skills as an open cross-platform standard (agentskills.io).

OpenAI released the Responses API and open-source Agents SDK in March 2025, then at DevDay (Oct 6, 2025) launched AgentKit — Agent Builder, ChatKit, Connector Registry and expanded Evals — to move agents from prototype to production.
Conductor, the parallel coding-agent desktop app (YC S24), reports use at companies including Linear, Vercel, Notion and Stripe and announced a Series A on Mar 30, 2026.
LangChain, maker of the LangGraph agent-orchestration runtime, reached unicorn status with a $125M Series B at a $1.25B valuation in Oct 2025, led by IVP with CapitalG and Sapphire Ventures.

watch_list.name: Conductor
watch_list.why: Pioneer of the 'run a team of coding agents on your Mac' category; parallel agents in isolated worktrees, adopted at Linear, Vercel, Notion, Stripe.
watch_list.funding: YC Summer 2024; Series A announced Mar 30, 2026 (amount not confirmed in primary source)

watch_list.name: Augment Code (Intent)
watch_list.why: Intent is a 'post-IDE' agent-orchestration workspace using coordinator/implementor/verifier agent teams over a shared living spec; bring-your-own-agent (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode).
watch_list.funding: Augment backed by ~$472M total (incl. Sutter Hill Ventures); Intent in public beta

watch_list.name: LangChain
watch_list.why: Most widely adopted open-source agent framework; LangGraph is a leading multi-agent orchestration/memory runtime and LangSmith leads agent evals/observability.
watch_list.funding: $125M Series B at $1.25B valuation (Oct 2025), led by IVP

payload.impact_so_far: In roughly 18 months the AI-agent stack produced a set of durable, fast-standardizing developer primitives — MCP for connectivity, Agent Skills for portable expertise, subagents and orchestration apps for parallel work, and SDKs plus evals for governance — now backed by both major labs and a wave of well-funded startups.
payload.watch_for_next: Watch for consolidation around open standards (MCP and Skills now under foundation/shared governance), multi-agent 'post-IDE' orchestration workspaces becoming the default interface, and evals/agent governance maturing into a required production discipline.
flags: Conductor Series A dollar amount unverified against a primary source (YC page confirms the round exists, dated Mar 30, 2026, but not the amount).
MCP co-creator attributions sourced from the a16z 'MCP co-creator' podcast; the official Anthropic MCP announcement does not name individuals.
